title: preprocessing not working with stable buildah Image
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

`buildah bud Dockerfile.in . `
using the prebuilt stable Dockerimage 
results in 
`exec: "cpp": executable file not found in $PATH`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. checkout buildah repo
2. `cd buildah/tests/bud/preprocess`
3.  create test Dockerfile 
```
echo 'FROM quay.io/buildah/stable' > test.dockerfile
echo 'COPY . .' >> test.dockerfile
echo 'RUN buildah bud  -f Decomposed.in .' >> test.dockerfile
```
4. check whether the normal decomposed test works: 
```
buildah bud  -f Decomposed.in .
STEP 1: FROM alpine:latest
STEP 2: RUN touch /etc/hello-world.txt
STEP 3: RUN echo "Hello world!"
Hello world!
STEP 4: RUN apk update
^C
```
-> yes, works perfectly fine.
5. check whether the buildah stable image works as intended
```
buildah bud  -f test.dockerfile .
STEP 1: FROM quay.io/buildah/stable
STEP 2: COPY . .
STEP 3: RUN buildah bud  -f Decomposed.in .
exec: "cpp": executable file not found in $PATH
time="2022-03-15T09:29:54Z" level=error msg="exit status 125"
error building at STEP "RUN buildah bud  -f Decomposed.in .": error while running runtime: exit status 125
ERRO[0003] exit status 125
```
-> broken, since cpp not found.

**Describe the results you received:**
The image was not built, because cpp(1) is not installed (or missing in the PATH environment variable).
`exec: "cpp": executable file not found in $PATH`

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect that the image would have been built using the stable buildah image as a builder image.

**possible solutions**
Installing cpp inside the image should solve the issue.
Drawback: The imagesize will be increased.

comments.body:
@Basti-D I think this can be done in a custom image where base is `FROM quay.io/buildah/stable` and `cpp` can be installed manually in additional step. I'm not sure if its a good idea to install `cpp` by default, since its not mandatory for `builds` and not sure if lot of users are using it.

But lets keep this issue open and see what other maintainers think.

```
$ rpm -q buildah --suggests
cpp
qemu-user-static
```
It is under suggests for RPM packaging. We could make the error message a little more clear.


`exec: "cpp": executable file not found in $PATH, please install the cpp package.`

> I'm not sure if its a good idea to install cpp by default, since its not mandatory for builds and not sure if lot of users are using it.

I think it's a bad user experience if a supported and documented feature of Buildah does not work with the official Buildah image.

It's _our_ responsibility not the users' to make that work. I am not worried at all about binary size as it's less than 900K.

We just need to update the Dockerfiles in https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/main/contrib/buildahimage and install `cpp` along with the other packages.

> ```
> $ rpm -q buildah --suggests
> cpp
> qemu-user-static
> ```
> 
> It is under suggests for RPM packaging. We could make the error message a little more clear.
> 
> `exec: "cpp": executable file not found in $PATH, please install the cpp package.`

Opened a PR to also address the error message: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3825
